events.event_description: Abraham Hanson presented credentials as Commissioner and Consul General, establishing the first U.S. diplomatic presence in Liberia. The title 'Commissioner' indicates a liaison/diplomatic agent role rather than a full legation.

evidence.quote: Abraham Hanson, Commissioner and Consul General. Appointed: 1863-06-08. Credentials presented: 1864-02-23.

events.event_description: John Seys presented credentials as Minister Resident/Consul General, elevating U.S. representation to a legation-level mission.

evidence.quote: John Seys, Minister Resident/Consul General. Appointed: 1866-10-08. Credentials presented: 1867-01-02.

events.event_description: Edward Richard Dudley was promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary, elevating the U.S. mission from a legation to an embassy.

evidence.quote: Edward Richard Dudley, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1948-08-11. Credentials presented: 1948-10-18. Promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary 1949-05-06.
